pull.title: [fix]deepgemm precompile when warmup
pull.body: ## Motivation I encountered a compilation error when starting the P-node with the --enable-dp-attention parameter. Specifically: ``` bash File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 256, in deep_gemm_execution_hook _maybe_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all(kernel_type, n, k, num_groups) File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 108, in _maybe_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all _compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all( File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 136, in _compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all executor = _BaseWarmupExecutor.create( ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 158, in create return { ^ File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 215, in __init__ self.lhs_q, self.lhs_s = _empty_token_fp8((max_m, k)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/deep_gemm_wrapper/compile_utils.py", line 171, in _empty_token_fp8 torch.empty(size, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float8_e4m3fn), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ ``` Then, when starting the service, try using "--skip-server-warmup" and manually simulate sending different warmup request input_ids (located in the `execute_server_warmup` method in `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/http_server.py`) , for example: ```bash curl -X POST "http://0.0.0.0:30011/generate" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{ "sampling_params": { "temperature": 0.0, "max_new_tokens": 8, "ignore_eos": true }, "bootstrap_host": ["2.2.2.2", "2.2.2.2"], "bootstrap_room": [0, 4611686018427387905], "input_ids": [[34, 101, 203, 309], [34, 101, 203, 309]] }' ``` When I switched between different input_ids, I found that certain requests would not produce errors. So I followed this approach, adding log entries to debug the issue, and discovered that when m=0 into function _maybe_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all warmup deepgemm precompile would result in an error. normal: error: ### A more complete normal log : ```bash [2025-12-26 08:51:25] INFO: Started server process [159737] [2025-12-26 08:51:25] INFO: Waiting for application startup. [2025-12-26 08:51:25] The server is fired up and ready to roll! [2025-12-26 08:51:25] INFO: Application startup complete. [2025-12-26 08:51:25] INFO: Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:30011 (Press CTRL+C to quit) [2025-12-26 08:51:29 DP0 TP1 EP1] Prefill batch, #new-seq: 2, #new-token: 8, #cached-token: 0, full token usage: 0.00, swa token usage: 0.00, #running-req: 0, #queue-req: 0, #prealloc-req: 0, #inflight-req: 0, input throughput (token/s): 0.00, [2025-12-26 08:51:29 DP0 TP2 EP2] Prefill batch, #new-seq: 2, #new-token: 8, #cached-token: 0, full token usage: 0.00, swa token usage: 0.00, #running-req: 0, #queue-req: 0, #prealloc-req: 0, #inflight-req: 0, input throughput (token/s): 0.00, [2025-12-26 08:51:29 DP0 TP3 EP3] Prefill batch, #new-seq: 2, #new-token: 8, #cached-token: 0, full token usage: 0.00, swa token usage: 0.00, #running-req: 0, #queue-req: 0, #prealloc-req: 0, #inflight-req: 0, input throughput (token/s): 0.00, [2025-12-26 08:51:29 DP0 TP0 EP0] Prefill batch, #new-seq: 2, #new-token: 8, #cached-token: 0, full token usage: 0.00, swa token usage: 0.00, #running-req: 0, #queue-req: 0, #prealloc-req: 0, #inflight-req: 0, input throughput (token/s): 0.00, [2025-12-26 08:51:30 DP0 TP0 EP0] Entering DeepGEMM JIT Pre-Compile session. It may take a long time (typically 10-20 mins) if you have not run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. It is recommended to run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm` with same args as `sglang.launch_server` for pre-compilation to reduce the overhead if you have not run it before. For example: `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 --tp 8 --trust-remote-code` [2025-12-26 08:51:30 DP0 TP0 EP0] Try DeepGEMM JIT Compiling for N=3392, K=4096, num_groups=1 with all Ms. It only takes a little time (typically 1 sec) if you have run `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. [2025-12-26 08:51:30 DP0 TP0 EP0] [_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all], kernel_type=, m_list=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ... , 16383, 16384], n=3392, k=4096, num_groups=1 [2025-12-26 08:51:30 DP0 TP0 EP0] [create][_BaseWarmupExecutor] kernel_type=, kwargs={'max_m': 16384, 'n': 3392, 'k': 4096, 'num_groups': 1} [2025-12-26 08:51:30 DP0 TP0 EP0] [init][_NormalWarmupExecutor] max_m=16384, n=3392, k=4096, num_groups=1 DeepGEMM warmup: 0%| | 0/16384 [00:00 N=4096, K=4096, num_groups=1 with all Ms. It only takes a little time (typically 1 sec) if you have run `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all], kernel_type=, m_list=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ... , 16383, 16384], n=4096, k=4096, num_groups=1 [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [create][_BaseWarmupExecutor] kernel_type=, kwargs={'max_m': 16384, 'n': 4096, 'k': 4096, 'num_groups': 1} [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [init][_NormalWarmupExecutor] max_m=16384, n=4096, k=4096, num_groups=1 DeepGEMM warmup: 0%| | 0/16384 [00:00 N=4096, K=2048, num_groups=1 with all Ms. It only takes a little time (typically 1 sec) if you have run `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all], kernel_type=, m_list=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...., 16383, 16384], n=4096, k=2048, num_groups=1 [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [create][_BaseWarmupExecutor] kernel_type=, kwargs={'max_m': 16384, 'n': 4096, 'k': 2048, 'num_groups': 1} [2025-12-26 08:51:31 DP0 TP0 EP0] [init][_NormalWarmupExecutor] max_m=16384, n=4096, k=2048, num_groups=1 DeepGEMM warmup: 0%| | 0/16384 [00:00 N=3712, K=4096, num_groups=1 with all Ms. It only takes a little time (typically 1 sec) if you have run `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. [2025-12-26 08:51:32 DP0 TP0 EP0] [_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all], kernel_type=, m_list=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ..., 16382, 16383, 16384], n=3712, k=4096, num_groups=1 [2025-12-26 08:51:32 DP0 TP0 EP0] [create][_BaseWarmupExecutor] kernel_type=, kwargs={'max_m': 16384, 'n': 3712, 'k': 4096, 'num_groups': 1} [2025-12-26 08:51:32 DP0 TP0 EP0] [init][_NormalWarmupExecutor] max_m=16384, n=3712, k=4096, num_groups=1 DeepGEMM warmup: 0%| | 0/16384 [00:00 [2025-12-26 08:51:34 DP0 TP0 EP0] [grouped_gemm_nt_f8f8bf16_contig with]m=256,n=4096,k=4096,num_groups=32,kernel_type= [2025-12-26 08:51:34 DP0 TP0 EP0] Entering DeepGEMM JIT Pre-Compile session. It may take a long time (typically 10-20 mins) if you have not run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. It is recommended to run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm` with same args as `sglang.launch_server` for pre-compilation to reduce the overhead if you have not run it before. For example: `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 --tp 8 --trust-remote-code` [2025-12-26 08:51:34 DP0 TP0 EP0] Try DeepGEMM JIT Compiling for N=4096, K=4096, num_groups=32 with all Ms. It only takes a little time (typically 1 sec) if you have run `python3 -m sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. [2025-12-26 08:51:34 DP0 TP0 EP0] [_compile_deep_gemm_one_type_all], kernel_type=, m_list=[128, 256, 384, 512, 640, 768, 896, 1024, 1152, 1280, 1408, 1536, 1664, 1792, 1920, 2048, 2176, 2304, 2432, [2025-12-26 08:58:30] The server is fired up and ready to roll! [2025-12-26 08:58:30] INFO: Application startup complete. It may take a long time (typically 10-20 mins) if you have not run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm`. It is recommended to run `sglang.compile_deep_gemm` with same args as `sglang.launch_server` for pre-compilation to reduce the overhead if you have not run it before.
